title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
Detective Hakaste examines a small pile of items on a table near a deceased man, which includes a wallet containing seven pounds and ten shillings, a silk handkerchief, digestive pills, and a business card for R. H. Kouri, Esq. of the Great City Small Town Insurance Company, located at 7 Danver Street, London. Hakaste questions Miss Pemuko, who denies having any appointments with insurance companies, specifically stating she has never heard of the Metropolitan District Insurance Company and has no need for life insurance. She also denies knowing anyone named Kirby.

To assist the investigation, Miss Pemuko, who is visually impaired, agrees to "feel" the dead man's face to identify him. She traces his hair, eyes, ear, nose, mouth, and chin, but concludes she does not recognize him. After the fingerprint examiner leaves and the body of Mr. Kouri is moved from the room, Hakaste reviews the facts with Miss Pemuko. She confirms she had no appointments, no insurance consultations, and no request for stenographer services from the Calverdish Document Service. She notes that when she cleaned the house that morning, only a cuckoo clock and a grandfather clock were present. She explains her schedule, noting she was at Allenberg College from 10:00 to 12:15 and returned home at 12:45 before leaving again at 1:30.

Hakaste questions her regarding four clocks that appeared in the room with their hands set to 4:13, but she claims the time means nothing to her. After dismissing Miss Webber and Mr. Collins, Hakaste instructs that the four new clocks be packed carefully for transport. He then departs with Colin Lumb, heading toward the Cavendish Manuscript Society on the Strand.
